seo.title: SaaS Applications implementation checklist
seo.description: A practical checklist for shipping SaaS Applications projects safely.

content.intro: A production-readiness checklist for SaaS applications focusing on multi-tenant security, billing integrity, and operational scaling for modern tech stacks.

items.title: Enforce Row-Level Security (RLS)
items.description: Verify that every database table containing tenant data has RLS enabled with a policy that checks the authenticated user's organization_id.

items.title: Cross-Tenant Access Testing
items.description: Execute a test suite that attempts to read/write a resource ID from Tenant A using an authentication token belonging to Tenant B; ensure it returns a 403 or 404.

items.title: JWT Claim Validation
items.description: Ensure your auth provider (Clerk/Auth.js) includes the current organization_id in the JWT claims to avoid extra database lookups for permissions.

items.title: Tenant-Aware Logging
items.description: Configure your logging middleware to append organization_id to every log entry to simplify troubleshooting during customer support tickets.

items.title: Soft-Delete Implementation
items.description: Implement a deleted_at timestamp on primary entities to allow for data recovery in case of accidental tenant-wide deletions.

items.title: Webhook Idempotency
items.description: Implement a check against an 'event_logs' table to ensure Stripe or LemonSqueezy webhook events are only processed once based on their unique event ID.

items.title: Subscription State Sync
items.description: Verify that the application UI and API middleware correctly handle 'past_due' and 'canceled' statuses by restricting access to premium features immediately.

items.title: Usage-Based Metering Accuracy
items.description: If using metered billing, implement a reconciliation script that compares internal usage logs against the billing provider's recorded usage every 24 hours.

items.title: Grace Period Logic
items.description: Configure a 3-7 day grace period for failed payments where the user sees a notification banner but retains access before the account is locked.

items.title: Tax Compliance Configuration
items.description: Enable automatic tax calculation (Stripe Tax/TaxJar) for all active jurisdictions to ensure VAT and Sales Tax are collected at checkout.

items.title: Token Usage Hard Caps
items.description: Set a maximum token limit per request in the OpenAI/Anthropic SDK calls to prevent a single runaway recursive loop from depleting your credits.

items.title: Per-Tenant Cost Attribution
items.description: Tag every AI API call with metadata containing the tenant_id to generate internal reports on which customers are most/least profitable.

items.title: Model Fallback Strategy
items.description: Implement a try/catch block that switches from a primary high-cost model to a lower-cost fallback model (e.g., GPT-4 to GPT-3.5-Turbo) if rate limits are hit.

items.title: Response Timeout Handling
items.description: Set a strict 30-second timeout for AI streaming responses to prevent Vercel/Edge function execution limits from being exceeded.

items.title: Prompt Versioning
items.description: Move prompt strings out of hardcoded logic and into a database or config file to allow updates without requiring a full code redeploy.

items.title: Environment Variable Audit
items.description: Scan the repository to ensure no production keys (STRIPE_SECRET, DATABASE_URL) are committed to Git; verify they are only in the CI/CD environment.

items.title: PII Encryption at Rest
items.description: Identify fields containing Personally Identifiable Information (PII) and ensure they are encrypted using a standard like AES-256 before being stored.

items.title: Rate Limiting Policy
items.description: Apply a global rate limit (e.g., 100 requests per minute per user) using Upstash or Redis to prevent API abuse and DDoS attacks.

items.title: GDPR Data Export Tool
items.description: Create an internal script or admin endpoint that aggregates all data for a specific user_id into a JSON file to satisfy 'Right to Access' requests.

items.title: Security Headers Configuration
items.description: Verify that the production server sends CSP, X-Content-Type-Options, and Strict-Transport-Security headers in every response.

items.title: Error Grouping and Alerting
items.description: Configure Sentry to alert the engineering team via Slack only when an error occurs more than 5 times in a 10-minute window to avoid noise.

items.title: Database Connection Pooling
items.description: If using Serverless (Vercel/AWS Lambda), ensure a connection pooler like Prisma Accelerate or Supabase Pooling is active to prevent 'Too many connections' errors.

items.title: Health Check Endpoint
items.description: Expose a /api/health endpoint that performs a 'SELECT 1' on the database and a ping to the cache to verify the full stack is operational.

items.title: Background Job Monitoring
items.description: Set up a Dead Letter Queue (DLQ) for failed transactional emails or background tasks to allow for manual retries of failed messages.

items.title: Performance Baseline
items.description: Record the LCP (Largest Contentful Paint) and API response time for the main dashboard to serve as a benchmark for future regressions.
